key_insight: CADMM-Prox adds a bi-level proximal mechanism to consensus ADMM to stabilize distributed optimization for non-smooth, non-convex problems and obtain convergence to a neighborhood of a Clarke stationary point.
engineering_relevance: The method is relevant for large-scale distributed learning, control, and signal-processing pipelines where sparse or inherently non-convex objectives must be optimized reliably across agents.
method: Combines classical Consensus ADMM with an outer-level proximal term.
Uses a sufficiently large proximal parameter to improve stability in the non-smooth, non-convex setting.
Assumes local objectives are semi-convex to support global convergence guarantees.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on phase retrieval, where CADMM-Prox showed more stable convergence than the baseline method.
watch_item: The guarantee is only convergence to a neighborhood of a Clarke stationary point, so exact stationarity is not ensured and performance may depend on proximal tuning.
paper_title: CADMM-Prox: A Bi-level Consensus ADMM for Non-smooth Non-convex Distributed Consensus Optimization
